name: Panhu
primary_sources: Christie, Anthony (1968). Chinese Mythology. Feltham: Hamlyn Publishing.
Yang, Lihui, et al. (2005). Handbook of Chinese Mythology. New York: Oxford University Press.

distinctive_features: human body with dog head in some variants

domain_specific_functional.mythological_role: married a princess and fathered 12 clans
domain_specific_functional.cultural_hero: worshiped by She and Yao people

relationships.description: Panhu married the princess after fulfilling the king's challenge.
Panhu and the princess had six sons and six daughters who became the 12 clans.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: important origin myth for various ethnic groups
primary_sources: Christie, Anthony (1968). Chinese Mythology. Feltham: Hamlyn Publishing.
Yang, Lihui, et al. (2005). Handbook of Chinese Mythology. New York: Oxford University Press.
